blockingIssues.description: Environment variables in McpServerConfig.env are never applied when spawning the server process. McpClient::connect() calls tokio::process::Command::new() and .args() but never calls .envs() or .env(). The env HashMap is parsed and stored but silently ignored at runtime.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented external MCP server dependency URL (https://github.com/wrale/mcp-server-tree-sitter) for tree-sitter integration

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document test module naming conventions within #[cfg(test)] blocks - current codebase uses `mod tests {` but lacks consistency guidance
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-treesitter-integration used `mod mcp_treesitter_tests {` which differs from existing patterns

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in MCP Configuration section whether env vars are required vs optional, and how relative command paths should be resolved
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-server-config implementation shows env field exists in config but was never passed to process

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on tokio::process::Command environment variable handling (.envs()/.env() methods) for MCP client process spawning
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-server-config reviewer identified that env handling was missing from Rust skill documentation
